body.text: Virginia hardware-ban bills on Gov. Spanberger's desk (deadline May 23, T-10); April NICS confirms politics-driven sales surge — VA +79% YoY, #2 long-gun nationally

The 2A storyline pivots to Virginia. Spanberger has 10 days to sign, veto, or let become law the legislature's 'assault firearms' sales ban (HB217/SB749) and carry ban (HB1524/SB727). April NICS released Tuesday PM confirms VA up nearly 79% YoY — #4 nationally, #2 long-gun. CT +28%, RI +54%.

facts.title: 1. VA hardware bans on Gov. Spanberger's desk + Apr NICS surge
2. DOJ vs. Denver + CO AR-15/mag suits (week 2)

body.text:
• VA bills on Gov. desk; decision deadline May 23 (T-10)
• April NICS: nationwide +1.6% YoY; pending-ban states surge
• DOJ CO suits in week 2 — D. Colo. scheduling-order watch
• ATF Sec. 926A transport NPRM — comment day 7 of 90
